title: Application for Recognition of Exemption Under 501(c)(3)
disclaimer: Self-help document service — NOT legal or tax advice. Eligibility for 1023-EZ vs full 1023, and the substance of the application, depend on the organization's facts; verify with the IRS and consult a licensed professional. You file with the IRS yourself.

fields.name: organization_name
fields.label: Organization legal name
fields.help: Exact legal name of the nonprofit corporation.

fields.name: ein
fields.label: EIN
fields.help: The organization must have an EIN before applying. Supplied transiently.

fields.name: formation_date
fields.label: Date of formation
fields.help: Date the organizing document was filed with the state.

fields.name: formation_state
fields.label: State of formation
fields.help: State where the nonprofit corporation was formed.

fields.name: variant
fields.label: Application variant
fields.help: Neutral list — the USER selects the variant they are eligible for. 1023-EZ has an eligibility worksheet in the IRS instructions. The tool does not decide eligibility for you.

fields.name: exempt_purpose
fields.label: Exempt purpose
fields.help: 501(c)(3) exempt-purpose category that matches the organization's mission.

fields.name: mission_narrative
fields.label: Mission / activities narrative
fields.help: Description of past, present, and planned activities.

fields.name: organizing_document_has_dissolution_clause
fields.label: Organizing document includes required dissolution clause?
fields.help: 501(c)(3) organizing documents generally must include purpose-limitation and dissolution clauses. See IRS instructions.

fields.name: officers
fields.label: Officers / directors / trustees
fields.help: Names and roles of the governing body.
